name: Tokyo
lifestyle: Dense cosmopolitan megacity with exceptional public transit, world-class dining, and universal healthcare. Central rents sit below SF/NY, while imported luxury goods and international school tuition drive the top tier. Common Fat FIRE visa routes are Highly Skilled Professional and Business Manager.

fx.note: USD per JPY, approx 149 JPY = 1 USD

true_fat_fire.description: Azabu/Hiroo/Aoyama 3-4BR tower with concierge. Michelin-track dinners weekly, omakase monthly, private car. 3-4 international trips plus domestic getaways annually.

guide.groceries: Premium depachika and Seijo Ishii-style imported baskets: top fruit, sashimi, wagyu, wine, and little need to watch the bill when stocking the kitchen.
guide.dining: Two to three polished dinners out most weeks, regular omakase or hotel dining, with nicer nights often around ¥15k-40k per person.
guide.rhythm: Mostly taxis or private car when convenient, concierge or housekeeping support, beauty and wellness spending, and a steady pace of galleries, hotels, and luxury shopping.

comfortable_expat.description: Shibuya/Meguro/Nakameguro 1-2BR mid-rise. Izakaya 2-3 nights/week with occasional omakase, trains plus taxis, one long international trip plus weekend onsen getaways.

guide.groceries: Neighborhood supermarkets plus depachika treats and imported staples: good produce, seafood, prepared foods, and pantry restocks without going fully luxury.
guide.dining: Izakaya or bistro 2-3 times a week plus one nicer meal every few weeks, roughly ¥2k-6k casual and ¥12k-20k when you splurge.
guide.rhythm: Mainly rail, occasional taxis, regular grooming and wellness, and weekend onsen or short domestic breaks rather than constant luxury logistics.

luxury_family.description: Hiroo/Motoazabu 3-4BR premium tower, weekly housekeeping, on-call driver. Top international school access (ASIJ / Nishimachi) when dependents apply, two international trips plus ski/beach breaks.

guide.groceries: Premium family basket with high-quality fruit, seafood, bentos, imported snacks, and frequent household provisioning from convenience-first supermarkets and food halls.
guide.dining: Family meals out most weekends plus adult date nights, with casual family meals often around ¥4k-8k per person and special dinners materially higher.
guide.rhythm: Weekly housekeeping, on-call driver support, after-school activities, and a calendar that assumes ski or beach breaks plus premium kid logistics.
